title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Go Client Systems · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for reusable client architecture, transport abstractions, middleware, and configuration, producing small composable client library boundary. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http small composable client library boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compose authentication, retries, metrics, tracing, caching, and redaction with documented ordering and ownership. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Configuration Secret Sources
steps.instruction:
review case — Go security handoff client library needs base authority, injected transport, auth, retry, cache, metrics, redaction, and typed operation G-5. Order wrappers, separate per-request state, validate configuration, and keep protocol plumbing private. Competency probe: Validate environment, file, runtime, and secret-provider configuration with precedence, rotation, and safe diagnostics. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
